channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.description:
Left on the path way next to a big tree that splits into a V shape at the base.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beckenham Place Park, Beckenham, Greater London

item.title: Abandoned vehicle., 14th June
item.description:
This is called a Irish brunaround car you get everything free.authority do nothing commissioners camera waste of time on lamp post waste of resources waste of money. This country is lawless now.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Merriam Avenue, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped mirror, 14th June
item.description:
Glass mirror dumped on pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 12, Matham Grove, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Middle of road on Templewood avenue, 14th June
item.description:
Following works on the road several weeks ago, a traffic management barrier and cones have remained positioned in the centre of the carriageway, apparently surrounding a utility or highway works excavation. Despite the prolonged presence of these obstructions, there appears to have been little or no visible progress towards completing the works and reinstating the road surface. The barrier significantly narrows the carriageway and obstructs the normal flow of traffic, creating unnecessary congestion and inconvenience for residents, visitors, delivery vehicles and emergency services. Given that Templewood Avenue is a residential road with regular vehicle movements, the continued occupation of the highway without apparent activity is becoming increasingly frustrating.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 33, Templewood Avenue, West Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Worn out centre line on Pelham Street, 14th June
item.description:
The nature of this junction is that cars enter Pelham Street from Old Brompton Road at considerable speed, and with a lack of centre line they are usually on the wrong side of the road. A reapplied centre line would reduce the number of cars on the wrong side of the road, and thereby reduce the likelihood of an accident.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 9, Pelham Street, Brompton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Suitcases pallet table & chair and fridge, 14th June
item.description:
Dumped in car parking space

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 5, Millfields Cottages, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Early this morning I found this lime bikes all surrounded around my car and has caused damage to my vehicle. I literally had to remove over eight bikes out of the way before I could move my car. These bikes are causing obstructions to people's daily lives., 14th June
item.description:
Line bike company leaving loads of bikes everywhere in unsafe manor causing obstruction.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Semley Gate, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken uneven pavement, 14th June
item.description:
Hi , this has been a on going issue for years, my dad's uses a wheel chair and mobility scooter there is no safe way for people in wheelchairs or on mobility scooters to make safe access down upland road without having to use the main road which is unsafe as there is on comming cars going up and down, please can you get this resolved before someone has a serious road accident and the broken pavement is a massive trip hazard to anyone going in and out of the cemetery gate

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4, Upland Road, Blendon, Bexleyheath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fleet Street / Chancery Lane junction pothole, 14th June
item.description:
The photo may not do justice to the depth of this pothole It's on the eastbound (north) side of Fleet Street at the junction with Chancery Lane Really nasty

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 192, Fleet Street, City of London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fence panels and mattress blocking access to bonito mews, 14th June
item.description:
Fence panels and mattress blocking access to bonito mews for cars and dustbin trucks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bonita Mews, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Can Wayne Hilton head of antisocial behavior who does nothing please address this issue of drug users taking drugs and injecting in the public park. In front of members of public. Drug users loitering in bushes getting their fix., 14th June
item.description:
If the new mayor of Hackney could please look into this as well and come up with some ideas to stop this happening seeing as it is a public space area.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Footpaths Across Mabley Green, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: E bikes parked blocking access, 14th June
item.description:
Bikes have been continuously parked on the path and outside of the parking bay blocking access. I have emailed Southwark highways six times and had no response.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St Asaph Road, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Coping on top of small wall at foot path to car park., 14th June
item.description:
The coping on wall to car park lose and dangerous, leading to carpark.its back door on the path at Danny Mccarthy court. Please fix. thank you .coping is on top of small wall
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4A, Odessa Street, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: South side of High Holborn, 14th June
item.description:
Really nasty pothole - enough to damage a car Or throw a cyclist off their bicycle if they don't spot it in time

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 283-288, High Holborn, Holborn, Greater London

item.title: Lad fell in the river on a line bike last night, 14th June
item.description:
We saw a guy fall in on a bike and he left the bike In the canal
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Upper North Street, Poplar, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dear Zoë Garbett can you please address this issue of lime bikes being abandoned and left in careless ways causing obstruction to pathway. I am 80 years old and I have to use a zima frame and I struggled to pass these bikes that are blocking pathway. I sometimes have to even walk in the road just to get around the bikes which is dangerous. From what I can understand acne council have contract with this company that is doing all this bad to my life. This causes me so much distress I do not want to leave my house because I'm always thinking about blind bags blocking my path., 14th June
item.description:
Please Zoë Garbett can you come up with a solution to stop this happening. Hackney Council only care about investment,money not residents welfare.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Semley Gate, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Microwave dumped on pavement, Becket St, SE1, 14th June
item.description:
As photo shows, dumped microwave

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Becket Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Severe blocked road drain flooding the bus stop and pavement, 14th June
item.description:
The road drain (highway gully) located inside the bus stop cage directly outside my shop front is completely blocked and holding stagnant, foul-smelling water. ​Whenever it rains, the drain fails instantly, causing heavy flooding across the pavement. Because it is inside the bus stop, pulling buses splash this foul water directly onto the footway and towards the shop entrance, creating a severe slip hazard, a public health nuisance from the smell, and directly disrupting business trading. ​Please send a highways team with a jetter/gully sucker to clear the subsurface blockage. Photo attached.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4-5, Killyon Terrace, Clapham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Branches need clearing, 14th June
item.description:
These branches are causing an obstruction & need clearing from the verge/ pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 51, Hill Close, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
